metadata.summary: Supplier update case with deterministic cheapest-supplier picks.

appointments.chief_complaint: Concerned about high medication costs for inhalers
appointments.notes: Review cost-saving options for respiratory medications; patient expressed financial concerns. Copay confirmed at $25.

medical_records.subjective: Patient reports difficulty affording current Fluticasone and Montelukast, costing over $25 monthly.
medical_records.objective: Compared existing medications against the supplier catalog and selected the lowest-cost safe options.
medical_records.assessment: Cost analysis shows safe, equivalent alternatives available at lower cost from Indian suppliers.

metadata.summary: Supplier update case with deterministic cheapest-supplier picks.

metadata.summary: Drug interaction review with deterministic risk facts.

appointments.chief_complaint: Concern about starting new sleep medication with current antidepressant
appointments.notes: Scheduled for drug interaction review regarding proposed Zolpidem addition to current Bupropion regimen. Patient instructed to bring current medication list.

medical_records.subjective: Patient reports difficulty falling asleep for past 2 weeks, currently taking Bupropion for depression without issues. Denies palpitations or chest pain.
medical_records.objective: Interaction review for Bupropion + Zolpidem: severity=low, risk_score=2, action=No significant interaction expected; observe for insomnia or agitation.
medical_records.assessment: Low-risk interaction between Bupropion and Zolpidem; no significant pharmacokinetic interaction expected but monitor for additive CNS effects.
medical_records.plan: Proceed with Zolpidem at lowest dose; monitor for insomnia exacerbation or agitation. Follow up in 2 weeks via telehealth.

metadata.summary: Drug interaction review with deterministic risk facts.

appointments.chief_complaint: Concerns about adding sleep medication to current antidepressant
appointments.notes: Scheduled for drug interaction review. Patient to bring current medication list. Follow-up in 4 weeks if new symptoms arise.

medical_records.subjective: Patient reports chronic insomnia and is considering adding Zolpidem to current regimen. Currently taking Bupropion. Denies recent agitation but notes occasional restlessness.
medical_records.objective: Interaction review for Bupropion + Zolpidem: severity=low, risk_score=2, action=No significant interaction expected; observe for insomnia or agitation.
medical_records.assessment: Low-severity drug interaction (risk score 2) between Bupropion and Zolpidem; no significant interaction expected per review. Monitor for insomnia or agitation.
medical_records.plan: Approve Zolpidem 5mg as needed for sleep. Advise patient to monitor for increased insomnia or agitation and report any concerns. Schedule follow-up in 2 weeks.

metadata.summary: Regimen optimization case with deterministic costed medication components.

appointments.chief_complaint: Trouble affording and managing my diabetes and blood pressure medications
appointments.notes: Scheduled for comprehensive medication review to evaluate cost-saving opportunities and simplify regimen. Patient to bring current medication list and insurance card.

medical_records.subjective: Patient reports taking Metformin twice daily and Lisinopril once daily as prescribed, but expresses concern about high monthly medication costs (approximately $600) and occasionally missing doses due to separate refill schedules.
medical_records.objective: Compiled current regimen costs, pill burden, and two deterministic optimization options for comparison.
medical_records.assessment: Current regimen is effective for BP and glucose control but presents high out-of-pocket cost and suboptimal adherence due to separate fill schedules.
medical_records.plan: Present two optimization options to patient: 1) Cost-synchronized generic fill to reduce cost, 2) Adherence packaging to improve compliance. Obtain patient preference and process authorization.

notes: Patient currently fills Metformin and Lisinopril separately each month
Reports occasional missed doses due to separate refill dates

optimized_regimens.name: Cost-Synchronized Generic Fill
optimized_regimens.focus: Aligns fill schedules for both medications to a single monthly pharmacy visit while maintaining current formulary brands
synergy_notes: Reduces pharmacy visits from two to one per month, improving convenience
No change in medication cost but minimizes out-of-pocket expenses associated with multiple trips
Uses existing insurance coverage without requiring prior authorization

optimized_regimens.name: Adherence Packaging Option
optimized_regimens.focus: Provides medications in a unit-dose adherence blister pack to simplify daily regimen
synergy_notes: Unit-dose blister packaging organizes AM/PM doses for both medications in one container
Reduces risk of missed doses by simplifying daily routine
Maintains therapeutic equivalence with current regimen while improving adherence

metadata.summary: Family coordination case grounded in deterministic household records.

appointments.chief_complaint: Stomach flu symptoms in two children, worried about spreading
appointments.notes: Both parents and children (Daniel, Sofia, Mateo) attending; provider fluent in Spanish for family discussion

appointments.chief_complaint: Follow-up on care coordination for children's recovery
appointments.notes: Reviewing medication access, school re-entry protocols, and home health needs; care coordinator to confirm resource delivery

medical_records.subjective: Mother reports Daniel (5yo) and Sofia (3yo) had vomiting/diarrhea x2 days; Mateo (1yo) now symptomatic. Concerned about dehydration and school exposure risks.
medical_records.objective: Household coordination review for family members: daniel_morales_tracer_0001, sofia_morales_tracer_0001, mateo_morales_tracer_0001. Shared concerns triaged into one pediatric visit and one coordination follow-up.
medical_records.assessment: Household viral gastroenteritis outbreak confirmed; acute care triaged to pediatric visit, coordination needs to follow-up appointment
medical_records.plan: Coordinate school nurse clearance for Daniel/Sofia; arrange oral rehydration solution delivery; 48-hour follow-up for Mateo's status
